projects.name: Eraring Battery
projects.note: 700 MW / 3,160 MWh. Grid-forming capability. On former Eraring coal plant site.

projects.name: Waratah Super Battery
projects.note: Operating at 350 MW (41%) due to catastrophic transformer failure Oct 2025. Full 850 MW targeted end 2026.
projects.milestone: NEM's most powerful battery (when fully operational)

projects.name: Orana BESS
projects.note: Third-largest battery by storage capacity in the NEM.

projects.name: Liddell BESS
projects.note: Built on former Liddell coal plant site. Phase 1 commissioned, Phase 2 expected May 2026. NSW LTESA Round 2.

projects.name: Tomago BESS
projects.note: A$800M. Near Raymond Terrace in Hunter-Central Coast REZ. EPC: Fluence.

insights: Battery storage now meets 20%+ of early evening peak demand NEM-wide
SA batteries hit 40% share of evening demand — a world record
Big batteries have overtaken peaking gas generators in NSW and VIC evening peaks
Over 7 TWh of solar and wind was curtailed in 2025 — batteries can absorb much of this
10.6% of all NEM dispatch intervals in Q2 2025 were at zero or negative prices
Battery net revenue reached A$69.5M in Q4 2025, doubling from Q4 2023
2-hour batteries dominate 2026 builds; 4-hour becomes standard from 2027
NSW LDES tender contracted 1.17 GW / 12 GWh of 8-10 hour batteries for post-2028
By end 2027, NEM battery capacity will exceed 12 GW — approaching coal fleet capacity
Batteries are doing more damage to coal profits than to gas, by suppressing peak prices

key_questions.question: Will excess solar dissipate?
key_questions.answer: Not in the short term. Solar deployment continues to outpace battery additions. However, 4-hour and 8-hour batteries coming online from 2027 will absorb significantly more midday surplus. The NSW LDES tender alone adds 12 GWh of deep storage.

key_questions.question: Will grid constraints reduce?
key_questions.answer: Yes. Batteries co-located with solar farms (like New England BESS) and at former power stations (Eraring, Liddell) add demand during the day near congestion points, easing export constraints. However, transmission upgrades are still needed.

key_questions.question: Will a coal unit be forced out?
key_questions.answer: Very likely. Batteries are suppressing peak prices that coal needs to remain profitable. Origin has already extended Eraring coal closure but may accelerate it as battery revenue grows. AGL's conversion of Liddell from coal to battery is the template. Batteries may do more damage to coal profits than to gas.

key_questions.question: How much solar can NSW batteries absorb?
key_questions.answer: With 4.3 GW of battery capacity online by end 2027, NSW batteries can absorb roughly 4.3 GW during peak solar hours. NSW currently generates about 5-6 GW of solar (utility + rooftop) midday, with 1-2 GW being curtailed. The battery build-out will substantially reduce but not eliminate curtailment.
